title: Genesis: Joseph in Egypt
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of genesis: joseph in egypt and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study genesis: joseph in egypt and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: When Joseph revealed himself to his brothers in Genesis 45, what did he tell them about who had sent him to Egypt? Answer: Joseph told his brothers that it was God who had sent him to Egypt, not them, to preserve life.
segments.correct_answer: it was God who sent him to Egypt to preserve life, not his brothers
acceptable_variants: god sent him not his brothers
god used his brothers' sin to send him ahead to save lives
you did not send me here — god did

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The fourth son of Jacob whose tribe would carry the messianic line; in Genesis 44, Judah's intercession for Benjamin foreshadows the kingly role his line would hold
The severe food shortage in Canaan and Egypt that drove Jacob's family to Egypt; the setting for the Joseph narrative's climax and Israel's entry into Egypt
